body:
I really like Radix Themes, in majority of the cases I don't have to change the UI as much so having a simple way of using components without the bloat inside the repo is very helpful. 

Are you considering to add Radix Themes like version for Base UI? 

Thanks

## Other requests

- https://x.com/shafuxyz/status/1955248696272228675

comments.body: If you downvote this issue, I'd love to see some details on why. Similarly, if you really want this, I'd also love to know why, what your needs and limitations are?

Is this happening someday? Currently lack of theme support is major block for us to move from @radix-ui/themes to this. Looks promising, but copy pasting css and shadcn/ui way is suboptimal. @radix-ui/themes way was super elegant, would love to see a version of that here.
Shadcn now has full support for Base UI https://ui.shadcn.com/docs/components/base/accordion

While it's nice that Shadcn supports Base UI, it also enforces Tailwind usage, which might be a deal breaker. So something like Radix Themes still makes sense. For my case, we need to implement data-intensive apps in the back office, and not spending time on styling all the components helps.

We still have no plans for anything like Radix Themes / Material UI / Chakra / Mantine / Hero UI etc. But I do appreciate that some teams don't want to own styling.

> copy pasting css is suboptimal
> not spending time on styling all the components helps

I'm curious, for anyone interested in this, it sounds like versioned/maintained CSS is what you want, is that correct? Or is it a full-blown component lib with high-level wrappers/abstractions that you want?

I have 2 use cases where I use `base-ui`:

1. "user-facing" app (actually, it's a very complex Storybook addon) where I need a specific theme. I manage it by myself, writing styles as I need based on the requirements. Base-ui is perfect here.
2. "back-office" app. Where I don't have strong requirements/preferences around "how exactly" the UI looks. It should be just "good looking"; there are no strict requirements regarding the design system.

The second case is where the default theme is particularly handy. `base-ui` still requires some CSS to style the examples in the docs; this addition might be beneficial there.

I develop many internal tools where a clean almost invisible and uniform UI is essential.  Radix Themes excels in this area, allowing me to focus on the core functionality of my code rather than getting bogged down by UI details.  Currently, OpenAI and Vercel-style UI is very popular so out-of-the-box control suffices.  Having some accent colour or corner radius control that covers about 100% of my use cases is a bonus.

Even for client work, I rarely customise controls extensively. I prefer a clean consistent UI designed by experienced developers rather than reinventing the wheel. This approach results in lighter code.

I would love to see Base UI to have this variant.

labels.name: waiting for 👍
labels.description: Waiting for upvotes. Open for community feedback and needs more interest to be worked on.

labels.name: scope: new proposal
labels.description: Proposal to increase the product scope covered.

title: [discussion] Add Radix Themes like version
